text: The word used in the Hebrew text seems to mean ‘liquid’, although the reckoning is made by weight. The figures here only correspond roughly with those in the original, which are besides uncertain; some think that the weight was twice that here indicated.
The Hebrew text adds at the end, ‘and the basin and its stand’.

This appears to be the sense of the Latin; the Hebrew text is more naturally interpreted as meaning ‘The man who either makes oil like this, or gives awaythis oil to a stranger’. The stranger, in that case, would be anyone not of the priestly family, as in 29.33 above.
